microsoft_ft: Owned the **product roadmap** for Azure's resilience validation platform, defining KPIs, prioritizing adjacent capabilities, and aligning engineering and business stakeholders to deliver **$14M+ impact** across enterprise customers.

Led **0→1 development** of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, translating customer needs into product requirements and self-service scheduling specs that cut operational toil by 30% and enabled a scalable, repeatable execution model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing program, defining performance targets and success criteria that achieved a **94% recovery rate** and surfaced critical hardware defects within a 4-month delivery cycle.

Directed 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility, including serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, aligning engineering, ops, and business teams.

Built an internal AI agent to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by **39%** and increasing program capacity by 21% — directly expanding the product's scalability without added headcount.
microsoft_2023: Shaped the **product roadmap** by conducting 11+ user interviews with Azure service teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing prioritization of intent-based YAML generation as a new product capability.

Drove **product adoption** for AI-powered code generation workflows, delivering demos and training across 14 key teams — translating a complex technical capability into a clear customer value proposition that saved 37 engineering hours monthly.

Improved retrieval performance and data integrity by defining metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search tool, reducing lookup time by **83%** and supporting scalable knowledge infrastructure.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across 140+ teams on a unified automation prioritization framework, **accelerating region launches by 28%** and generating $3M in revenue — a direct result of product strategy and stakeholder execution.

Conducted discovery with 20+ service teams to quantify 81 hours of manual effort per region launch, then built a **product roadmap** to automate critical paths, framing the business case with unit economics and cost-reduction targets.

Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to track operational toil across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to identify automation gaps, prioritize high-impact investments, and validate make-vs.-automate trade decisions.
amazon_robotics: Defined the **migration product strategy** for a 2,000+ unit pilot, mapping hardware and software dependencies across 1,200+ stations to achieve zero operational downtime — balancing technical requirements with schedule and reliability targets.

Aligned cross-functional IT, Operations, and Engineering teams to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and establishing a repeatable release model for manufacturing-floor robotics systems.

Facilitated Agile sprint planning and backlog prioritization, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and maintain program velocity during a complex multi-team hardware-software transition.
pro_painters: Executed a **go-to-market strategy** combining CRM process redesign and digital channel optimization, reducing Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%, boosting conversions by 2.7%, and increasing job bookings by 26% across 200+ monthly proposals.

Built pricing and **unit economics models** by analyzing 1,000+ project records to identify margin leaks, delivering profitability insights that directly informed leadership's cost targets and scaling assumptions.

Owned end-to-end scoping and invoicing lifecycle, using CRM data to surface operational inefficiencies and recommend process improvements — mirroring the **product owner** mindset of translating field data into actionable product decisions.

tailoring_notes: Wove 'product roadmap' (JD: 'Define and own the product roadmap') into microsoft_ft bullet 1, microsoft_2023 bullet 1, and microsoft_2022 bullet 2 to directly mirror JD ownership language.

Used 'unit economics' and 'cost targets' (JD: 'Unit economics and cost models', 'performance, cost, and schedule targets') in microsoft_2022 bullet 2 and pro_painters bullet 2 to surface business-case credibility.

Used 'go-to-market strategy' (JD: 'Develop and execute the go-to-market strategy') verbatim in pro_painters bullet 1, and 'product owner' framing (JD: 'Serve as product owner') in pro_painters bullet 3.
Used 'KPI/KPP definition' and 'performance targets' (JD: 'Establish key performance metrics (KPPs/KPIs)') in microsoft_ft bullet 1 to show direct alignment with technical performance ownership.
Used 'customer value proposition' (JD: 'translating technical systems into customer-facing value propositions') in microsoft_2023 bullet 2 to address a required skill directly.
